title: Neumorphic Button
summary: A soft-UI button that appears extruded at rest and presses inward when active.
description: Uses the dual light/dark shadow neumorphism technique; on :active the shadows flip to inset so the button looks physically depressed.
performance_notes: Dual shadows + an inset swap on press; trivial.
accessibility_notes: Neumorphism's classic weakness is low contrast — make the label clearly readable and don't rely only on the shadow to signal it's a button. Keep a visible focus ring (neumorphism often forgets this).

customization.name: --bg
customization.description: Shared surface color (button + page must match)

variations: Toggle that stays pressed when active
ai_usage: Use in calm, tactile soft-UI dashboards — but ensure contrast and an explicit focus ring (neumorphism's main accessibility pitfalls). The button and its container MUST share --bg. Rest: box-shadow with a light offset (top-left) and dark offset (bottom-right). On :active: switch both to inset. Derive the light/dark shades from --bg. Add a focus-visible outline for keyboard users.
source:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
  <title>Neumorphic Button</title>
  <script type="application/json" id="effect-meta">
  {
    "id": "neumorphic-button",
    "title": "Neumorphic Button",
    "summary": "A soft-UI button that appears extruded at rest and presses inward when active.",
    "description": "Uses the dual light/dark shadow neumorphism technique; on :active the shadows flip to inset so the button looks physically depressed.",
    "theme": "glass-neumorphism",
    "categories": ["surface", "neumorphism", "button"],
    "tags": ["neumorphism", "soft-ui", "button", "press", "shadow", "monochrome"],
    "tech": ["css"],
    "era": "2020s",
    "difficulty": "beginner",
    "dependencies": [],
    "browser_support": "All browsers.",
    "performance_notes": "Dual shadows + an inset swap on press; trivial.",
    "accessibility_notes": "Neumorphism's classic weakness is low contrast — make the label clearly readable and don't rely only on the shadow to signal it's a button. Keep a visible focus ring (neumorphism often forgets this).",
    "customization": [
      { "name": "--bg", "description": "Shared surface color (button + page must match)", "default": "#e0e5ec" },
      { "name": "--accent", "description": "Label color", "default": "#5b3ee0" }
    ],
    "variations": ["Dark-mode neumorphism", "Circular icon button", "Toggle that stays pressed when active"],
    "related": ["neumorphism-card", "press-3d-button"],
    "ai_usage": "Use in calm, tactile soft-UI dashboards — but ensure contrast and an explicit focus ring (neumorphism's main accessibility pitfalls). The button and its container MUST share --bg. Rest: box-shadow with a light offset (top-left) and dark offset (bottom-right). On :active: switch both to inset. Derive the light/dark shades from --bg. Add a focus-visible outline for keyboard users."
  }
  </script>
  <style>
    :root { --bg: #e0e5ec; --light: #ffffff; --dark: #a3b1c6; --accent: #5b3ee0; }
    html, body { height: 100%; margin: 0; }
    body { display: grid; place-items: center; background: var(--bg); font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; }
    .neu-btn {
      border: none; cursor: pointer; background: var(--bg); color: var(--accent); font-weight: 800; font-size: 1rem;
      padding: 1rem 2.4rem; border-radius: 16px;
      box-shadow: 6px 6px 12px var(--dark), -6px -6px 12px var(--light);
      transition: box-shadow .15s ease;
    }
    .neu-btn:active { box-shadow: inset 6px 6px 12px var(--dark), inset -6px -6px 12px var(--light); }
    .neu-btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent); outline-offset: 4px; }
    @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .neu-btn { transition: none; } }
  </style>
</head>
<body>
  <button class="neu-btn">Press me</button>
</body>
</html>
